notes: Humblytics agent-signup flow: POST /api/v1/auth/agent-initiate → {token, verifyUrl, expiresAt} then GET /api/v1/auth/agent-signup-status?token= polls until {status:'complete', authToken}. The verifyUrl requires a HUMAN to open a browser, select a paid plan ($65–$79/mo), and complete Stripe checkout — the session expires in 10 minutes. The authToken is one-time-delivered (session deleted after first 'complete' read). There is NO way for a fully autonomous agent to complete signup without human payment. This contract is marked as requiring human assistance at the payment step. The agent_task initiates and then polls; it can only succeed if a human clicks the verifyUrl during the test window. We use the agent to handle the HTTP ceremony and report the authToken once the human completes payment. Subsequent steps (add-custom-site, generate api-key) are fully autonomous.

steps.description: Verifier probe: does the agent-reported authToken actually work?
Verifier probe: does the agent-reported apiKey work against the external analytics API?

assertions.description: agent received and reported the one-time authToken
agent created a property and reported its ID
agent generated and reported an API key
authToken is real and accepted by the API
apiKey is real and accepted by the external analytics API

steps.description: GET analytics summary — apiKey still valid
GET split-test recommendations for the homepage
POST /split-tests — create a no-code A/B test

assertions.description: experiment ID returned from split test creation
